title: G001: Implement Keystone dependency details
description:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** CC-0015 (S015)

## Summary

S015 completes the detailed dependency interactions for the Keystone reconciler. The core logic — DB connection string assembly (managed/brownfield), memcached server list discovery, Fernet rotation CronJob with writable Secret mount and maxActiveKeys enforcement, and bootstrap Job with admin credentials — was implemented in CC-0013. The remaining gaps are: (1) **Deployment rolling restart mechanism after Fernet key rotation** via a hash-based pod template annotation, (2) **standalone table-driven unit tests** for the pure helper functions (`resolveDatabaseHost`, `dbPort`, `resolveCacheServers`), and (3) **envtest integration tests** verifying CronJob and Job detailed specs.

## Scope

**Included:**
- Fernet keys hash annotation on Deployment pod template (`keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash`) — computed from the `{name}-fernet-keys` Secret data, set on `spec.template.metadata.annotations` in `buildKeystoneDeployment()` (currently has no annotations at line 87-89 of `reconcile_deployment.go`). The existing `secretToKeystoneMapper` watch already triggers reconcile on fernet-keys Secret changes (via UID ownership check at `keystone_controller.go:160-182`).
- Table-driven unit tests for `resolveDatabaseHost()` — managed mode (`{clusterRef.name}.{ns}.svc:{port}`), brownfield (`{host}:{port}`), port fallback to 3306
- Table-driven unit tests for `dbPort()` — explicit port, zero/default fallback to 3306
- Table-driven unit tests for `resolveCacheServers()` — managed mode pod DNS pattern (`memcached-{i}.{name}:11211`), brownfield explicit list join, replicas=0 fallback to 3, nil ClusterRef + empty Servers returning `""`
- Envtest integration test verifying CronJob detailed spec: schedule, init container `copy-keys`, emptyDir volume, `fernet-keys-src` Secret volume, ServiceAccount `{name}-fernet-rotate`, `OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys` env var, image tag
- Envtest integration test verifying bootstrap Job detailed spec: `keystone-manage bootstrap` command/args, admin-credentials env from SecretKeyRef, `backoffLimit: 4`, `ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 300`, config volume mount

**Excluded:**
- Refactoring existing sub-reconcilers (complete from CC-0013, YAGNI)
- Fernet key rotation E2E tests (covered by CC-0016 Chainsaw suite)
- Credential-keys volume mount management (noted as deferred in `reconcile_deployment.go:160`, separate feature)
- MariaDB CR status field resolution (current implementation uses service DNS `{name}.{ns}.svc`, which is the correct K8s pattern)
- ConfigMap hash annotation (already handled by content-hash ConfigMap naming in `reconcileConfig`)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph FernetRotation["Fernet Key Rotation Flow"]
        CJ["CronJob: fernet-rotate"]
        IC["Init Container: copy keys to emptyDir"]
        MC["Main Container: keystone-manage fernet_rotate"]
        PY["Python script: PATCH Secret via K8s API"]
        SEC["Secret: fernet-keys"]
        WATCH["secretToKeystoneMapper triggers reconcile"]
        REC["Reconciler: compute fernet-keys hash"]
        ANN["Deployment pod template annotation update"]
        ROLL["Rolling restart of Keystone pods"]
    end

    CJ --> IC
    IC --> MC
    MC --> PY
    PY -->|"PATCH"| SEC
    SEC -->|"ownership UID match"| WATCH
    WATCH --> REC
    REC -->|"hash changed"| ANN
    ANN --> ROLL

    subgraph DBResolution["DB Connection String Assembly"]
        MANAGED_DB["Managed: ClusterRef set"]
        BROWN_DB["Brownfield: Host set"]
        DNS["Service DNS: name.ns.svc:port"]
        EXPLICIT["Explicit: host:port"]
        CONN["mysql+pymysql://user:pass@host:port/db"]
    end

    MANAGED_DB --> DNS --> CONN
    BROWN_DB --> EXPLICIT --> CONN

    subgraph CacheResolution["Memcached Server Discovery"]
        MANAGED_MC["Managed: ClusterRef set"]
        BROWN_MC["Brownfield: Servers list"]
        POD_DNS["Pod DNS: memcached-i.name:11211"]
        EXPLICIT_MC["Explicit server list"]
        SERVERS["Comma-joined server string"]
    end

    MANAGED_MC --> POD_DNS --> SERVERS
    BROWN_MC --> EXPLICIT_MC --> SERVERS
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CJ as Fernet CronJob
    participant K8S as K8s API
    participant SEC as fernet-keys Secret
    participant REC as Keystone Reconciler
    participant DEP as Keystone Deployment

    CJ->>CJ: Init: copy Secret keys to emptyDir
    CJ->>CJ: keystone-manage fernet_rotate
    CJ->>K8S: PATCH Secret with rotated keys
    K8S->>SEC: Update Secret data
    SEC-->>REC: secretToKeystoneMapper watch fires
    REC->>SEC: Read Secret data, compute SHA-256 hash
    REC->>DEP: Set pod template annotation with new hash
    DEP-->>DEP: Rolling restart with new Fernet keys
```

## Key Components

- **`resolveDatabaseHost()`** (`reconcile_config.go:177`): Pure function resolving DB host — managed mode constructs `{clusterRef.name}.{ns}.svc:{port}`, brownfield uses explicit `{host}:{port}`. Implemented; needs standalone table-driven unit tests.
- **`dbPort()`** (`reconcile_config.go:188`): Pure function returning port with 3306 default fallback. Implemented; needs edge case unit tests (zero value, explicit value).
- **`resolveCacheServers()`** (`reconcile_config.go:155`): Pure function constructing memcached endpoint list — managed mode generates `memcached-{i}.{name}:11211` for `replicas` count (default 3), brownfield joins explicit `Servers` list. Implemented; needs standalone table-driven unit tests.
- **Fernet keys hash annotation** (NEW in `reconcile_deployment.go`): Compute SHA-256 of the `{name}-fernet-keys` Secret data, set as `keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash` annotation on `spec.template.metadata.annotations` in `buildKeystoneDeployment()`. Requires `reconcileDeployment()` to read the fernet-keys Secret and pass the hash. This is the missing piece — `buildKeystoneDeployment()` currently sets no pod template annotations (line 87-89).
- **`fernetRotationCronJob()`** (`reconcile_fernet.go:218`): Complete CronJob spec with init container, emptyDir, `OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys` env var, ServiceAccount. Implemented; needs envtest test verifying detailed spec fields.
- **`buildBootstrapJob()`** (`reconcile_bootstrap.go:69`): Complete Job spec with `backoffLimit: 4`, `ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 300`, admin-credentials env from SecretKeyRef, config ConfigMap volume mount. Implemented; needs envtest test verifying detailed spec fields.

stories.title: Keystone pods restart automatically after Fernet key rotation
stories.want: Keystone pods to perform a rolling restart when the fernet-keys Secret is updated by the rotation CronJob
stories.so_that: newly rotated Fernet keys are picked up by all Keystone API pods without manual intervention
criteria: buildKeystoneDeployment() sets annotation keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash on spec.template.metadata.annotations
Annotation value is a SHA-256 hex string computed from the fernet-keys Secret data map
When fernet-keys Secret data changes, the annotation value changes on next reconcile, triggering a rolling restart
reconcileDeployment() reads the fernet-keys Secret and passes the hash to buildKeystoneDeployment()

resolveCacheServers() tested with brownfield mode joining Servers list
resolveCacheServers() tested with replicas=0 defaulting to 3
resolveCacheServers() tested with nil ClusterRef and empty Servers returning empty string

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment() SHALL set a keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash annotation on spec.template.metadata.annotations, computed as the SHA-256 hex digest of the fernet-keys Secret data
requirements.rationale: Changing the pod template annotation causes Kubernetes to perform a rolling restart, ensuring pods pick up rotated Fernet keys

scenarios.name: Annotation set from Secret data
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment() is called with a fernetKeysHash string
scenarios.then: the Deployment spec.template.metadata.annotations contains keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash with the provided hash value

scenarios.name: Hash changes when Secret data changes
scenarios.when: the fernet-keys Secret data is modified by the rotation CronJob and reconcileDeployment() runs
scenarios.then: the annotation value on the Deployment pod template changes to the new hash

scenarios.name: Annotation is a valid SHA-256 hex string
scenarios.when: the annotation is set
scenarios.then: the value is a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string

requirements.description: reconcileDeployment() SHALL read the fernet-keys Secret and compute the SHA-256 hash to pass to buildKeystoneDeployment()
requirements.rationale: The reconciler must bridge the Secret data to the Deployment annotation to close the rolling restart loop

scenarios.name: Secret exists and hash is computed
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment() runs and the fernet-keys Secret exists
scenarios.then: the SHA-256 hash of the Secret data is computed and passed to buildKeystoneDeployment()

scenarios.name: Secret does not exist
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment() runs and the fernet-keys Secret does not yet exist
scenarios.then: an empty hash string is used (annotation set to empty or omitted) and reconciliation continues without error

requirements.description: resolveDatabaseHost() SHALL return {clusterRef.name}.{namespace}.svc:{port} in managed mode and {host}:{port} in brownfield mode
requirements.rationale: Correct database host resolution is critical for the Keystone connection string

scenarios.name: Managed mode with default port
scenarios.when: ClusterRef is set and Port is 0

scenarios.name: Managed mode with explicit port

scenarios.name: Brownfield mode with default port

requirements.description: dbPort() SHALL return the explicit port when > 0, or 3306 as default

scenarios.name: Explicit port
scenarios.when: spec.database.port is 3307

scenarios.name: Zero port (default)
scenarios.when: spec.database.port is 0

scenarios.name: Default value (not set)
scenarios.when: spec.database.port is not set (zero value for int32)

requirements.description: resolveCacheServers() SHALL construct memcached endpoint list in managed mode and join explicit servers in brownfield mode
requirements.rationale: Correct memcached endpoint discovery is required for Keystone token caching

scenarios.name: Managed mode with default replicas
scenarios.when: ClusterRef is set and Replicas is 0

scenarios.name: Managed mode with custom replicas
scenarios.when: ClusterRef is set and Replicas is 5

scenarios.name: Brownfield mode with server list
scenarios.when: Servers is [mc-0:11211, mc-1:11211]

scenarios.when: ClusterRef is nil and Servers is empty

scenarios.name: Single server brownfield
scenarios.when: Servers is [mc:11211]

requirements.description: An envtest integration test SHALL verify the Fernet rotation CronJob detailed spec fields after full reconciliation
requirements.rationale: Verifying CronJob spec in a real API server environment catches issues that fake-client unit tests miss (defaulting, admission, validation)

scenarios.name: CronJob spec matches expectations after full reconciliation
scenarios.when: full reconciliation completes to Ready=True in envtest
scenarios.then: the CronJob schedule, init container, main container, env vars, volumes, and ServiceAccount all match the expected values

scenarios.when: CronJob is created with spec.image.repository=ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone and spec.image.tag=2025.2

requirements.description: An envtest integration test SHALL verify the bootstrap Job detailed spec fields after full reconciliation
requirements.rationale: Verifying Job spec in a real API server environment catches issues with SecretKeyRef resolution, volume mounts, and Job lifecycle fields

scenarios.name: Job spec matches expectations after full reconciliation
scenarios.when: full reconciliation completes and bootstrap Job is created in envtest
scenarios.then: the Job command, args, env, backoffLimit, ttlSecondsAfterFinished, and volumes all match the expected values

scenarios.name: Admin password env uses SecretKeyRef
scenarios.when: bootstrap Job is created
scenarios.then: BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env var uses SecretKeyRef with name from spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef and key password

requirements.description: The fernet-keys hash computation SHALL produce a deterministic SHA-256 hex digest from the Secret data map
requirements.rationale: Deterministic hashing ensures the annotation only changes when Secret data actually changes, avoiding unnecessary rolling restarts

scenarios.name: Same data produces same hash
scenarios.when: the same Secret data map is hashed twice

scenarios.name: Different data produces different hash
scenarios.when: Secret data is modified (e.g., a key is rotated)

scenarios.name: Hash is order-independent for Secret data keys
scenarios.when: Secret data has keys in different insertion order
scenarios.then: the hash value is the same (JSON marshaling of map sorts keys)

tasks.title: Add fernet-keys hash annotation to buildKeystoneDeployment() (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Modify `buildKeystoneDeployment()` in `reconcile_deployment.go` to accept a `fernetKeysHash string` parameter and set annotation `keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash` on `spec.template.metadata.annotations`. Add a `fernetKeysHash()` helper function that reads the `{name}-fernet-keys` Secret, JSON-marshals the `.Data` map, and computes SHA-256 hex (following the pattern in `internal/common/job/job.go:PodSpecHash()`). Modify `reconcileDeployment()` to call `fernetKeysHash()` and pass the result to `buildKeystoneDeployment()`. If the Secret does not exist, pass an empty string. Update existing unit tests in `reconcile_deployment_test.go` to pass the new parameter and verify the annotation is present with a valid 64-char hex value.

tasks.title: Add table-driven unit tests for resolveDatabaseHost() and dbPort() (REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: Add table-driven tests to `reconcile_config_test.go` for `resolveDatabaseHost()` and `dbPort()`. Test cases for `resolveDatabaseHost()`: managed mode with default port (ClusterRef={Name:mariadb}, Port=0 → mariadb.default.svc:3306), managed mode with explicit port (Port=3307 → mariadb.default.svc:3307), brownfield with explicit port (Host=db.example.com, Port=3306 → db.example.com:3306), brownfield with default port (Host=db.example.com, Port=0 → db.example.com:3306). Test cases for `dbPort()`: explicit port (3307 → 3307), zero port (0 → 3306). Follow existing table-driven test pattern from `internal/common/config/config_test.go` using `NewGomegaWithT(t)` and `t.Run()`.

tasks.title: Add table-driven unit tests for resolveCacheServers() (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Add table-driven tests to `reconcile_config_test.go` for `resolveCacheServers()`. Test cases: brownfield with multiple servers ([mc-0:11211, mc-1:11211] → mc-0:11211,mc-1:11211), brownfield with single server ([mc:11211] → mc:11211), managed mode with default replicas (ClusterRef={Name:memcached}, Replicas=0 → memcached-0.memcached:11211,memcached-1.memcached:11211,memcached-2.memcached:11211), managed mode with custom replicas (Replicas=5 → 5 endpoints), neither ClusterRef nor Servers (→ empty string), managed mode with Replicas=1 (→ single endpoint). Follow table-driven pattern with `NewGomegaWithT(t)` and `t.Run()`.

tasks.title: Add envtest integration test for CronJob detailed spec (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Add `TestIntegration_CronJobDetailedSpec` to `integration_test.go`. The test creates a brownfield Keystone CR using `integrationBrownfieldKeystone()`, calls `createPrerequisites()`, drives full reconciliation via `driveFullReconciliation()`, then fetches the `{name}-fernet-rotate` CronJob and verifies: (1) schedule matches spec.fernet.rotationSchedule, (2) init container name=copy-keys with correct image and copy command, (3) init container has fernet-keys-src (ReadOnly) and fernet-keys volume mounts, (4) main container name=fernet-rotate with correct image and fernetRotateScript command, (5) env vars: SECRET_NAME={name}-fernet-keys, SECRET_NAMESPACE from fieldRef, OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys=3, (6) ServiceAccountName={name}-fernet-rotate, (7) volumes: fernet-keys-src (Secret) and fernet-keys (emptyDir). Follow existing envtest test pattern.

tasks.title: Add envtest integration test for bootstrap Job detailed spec (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Add `TestIntegration_BootstrapJobDetailedSpec` to `integration_test.go`. The test creates a brownfield Keystone CR, drives full reconciliation (but does NOT simulate bootstrap Job completion so the Job still exists), fetches the `{name}-bootstrap` Job and verifies: (1) container command=[keystone-manage, bootstrap], (2) args include --bootstrap-password $(BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD), --bootstrap-admin-url, --bootstrap-internal-url, --bootstrap-public-url, --bootstrap-region-id RegionOne, (3) BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env from SecretKeyRef with name=keystone-admin and key=password, (4) backoffLimit=4, (5) ttlSecondsAfterFinished=300, (6) config volume mount at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/ (ReadOnly), (7) config volume source is ConfigMap with name prefix {name}-config-. Use a modified `driveFullReconciliation` approach that stops before simulating bootstrap Job completion.

tasks.title: Update reference documentation for fernet-keys hash annotation (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Update `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` in the reconcileDeployment section to document the new `keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash` annotation on the Deployment pod template. Add a row to the Deployment Spec table for the annotation. Add a brief explanation in the reconcileDeployment section describing the rolling restart mechanism triggered by fernet-keys Secret changes. Reference CC-0015.

review_criteria: All existing unit tests pass: go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/ passes without regressions
All existing integration tests pass: go test -tags=integration ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/ passes without regressions
Fernet hash annotation is set correctly: Deployment pod template has keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash annotation with valid SHA-256 hex
Table-driven tests follow existing pattern: struct with name/input/want, t.Run(), NewGomegaWithT(t), same style as config_test.go and common/config/config_test.go
Envtest tests use existing helpers: setupEnvTestWithController, integrationBrownfieldKeystone, createPrerequisites, driveFullReconciliation
No scope creep: only files in reconcile_deployment.go, reconcile_config_test.go, reconcile_deployment_test.go, integration_test.go, and docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md are modified
go vet and golangci-lint pass without errors on the keystone operator module
Hash computation is deterministic: same Secret data always produces the same annotation value
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

The fernet-keys hash annotation closes the loop between the Fernet rotation CronJob and the Keystone Deployment:

```
CronJob PATCH → Secret update → secretToKeystoneMapper watch → Reconcile()
→ reconcileDeployment() reads Secret → computes hash → sets annotation
→ K8s rolling restart
```

## Key Decisions

1. **Hash computation function location**: Add `fernetKeysHash()` as a private method on `KeystoneReconciler` in `reconcile_deployment.go`. It reads the `{name}-fernet-keys` Secret, JSON-marshals the `.Data` map, and returns `hex.EncodeToString(sha256.Sum256(data)[:])`. This follows the pattern in `internal/common/job/job.go:PodSpecHash()` but operates on Secret data instead of PodSpec.

2. **buildKeystoneDeployment() signature change**: Add `fernetKeysHash string` parameter. All callers (reconcileDeployment and test helpers readyDeployment/notReadyDeployment) must be updated.

3. **Secret not found handling**: If the fernet-keys Secret doesn't exist yet (first reconcile before reconcileFernetKeys runs), return empty string. This is safe because reconcileFernetKeys runs before reconcileDeployment in the sequential reconciliation flow, but we handle it defensively.

4. **Deterministic hashing**: `json.Marshal` on `map[string][]byte` sorts keys alphabetically, ensuring deterministic output. No additional sorting needed.

## Files Modified

- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` — Add fernetKeysHash(), modify buildKeystoneDeployment() signature, modify reconcileDeployment() to read Secret
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go` — Update existing tests for new signature, add annotation and hash tests
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config_test.go` — Add TestResolveDatabaseHost, TestDBPort, TestResolveCacheServers
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go` — Add TestIntegration_CronJobDetailedSpec, TestIntegration_BootstrapJobDetailedSpec
- `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` — Document annotation in reconcileDeployment section

## Risks

- **Import cycle**: `reconcile_deployment.go` needs `crypto/sha256`, `encoding/hex`, `encoding/json` — all stdlib, no risk.
- **Existing test breakage**: `buildKeystoneDeployment()` signature change breaks all callers. Must update `readyDeployment()`, `notReadyDeployment()`, and all `TestReconcileDeployment_*` tests in one task.
- **Envtest flakiness**: Integration tests depend on timing. Use existing `eventuallyTimeout` and `pollInterval` constants for consistency.
